idea.title: Dark Mode Theme
idea.description: Add a dark mode toggle to the budget tracker app. Users should be able to switch between light and dark themes from Settings, and the preference should persist across app restarts.
features: Add dark theme definition to FortunaTheme
Add theme toggle in Settings screen
Apply theme dynamically across all screens using Provider
Follow Material 3 dark color scheme guidelines
out_of_scope: Scheduled/auto theme switching based on time of day

overview.problem: Users who prefer dark interfaces or use the app in low-light conditions experience eye strain and reduced battery life on OLED screens because the app only supports a light theme.
overview.solution: Add a full dark mode theme that users can toggle from Settings, with the preference persisted across app restarts.
overview.target_user: All Fortuna budget app users, especially those who use the app at night or prefer dark interfaces.
overview.success_metric: Users can toggle dark mode in Settings and the entire app responds immediately; preference persists after app restart.

screens.name: Settings Screen
screens.purpose: User selects theme preference (Light / Dark / System default).

features.name: Dark Theme Definition
features.description: Define a complete dark ColorScheme and ThemeData in FortunaTheme.dark following Material 3 dark theme guidelines. All surface, primary, secondary, error, and container colors must have dark variants.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app is running, when dark mode is active, then all screens use dark surface colors (dark backgrounds, light text).
Given the app is running, when dark mode is active, then the ColorScheme.brightness is Brightness.dark.
Given the app is running, when dark mode is active, then all Material 3 color tokens (surface, primary, secondary, error, outline, containers) have appropriate dark values.

features.name: Theme State Management
features.description: Create a ThemeModel (ChangeNotifier) that manages the current theme mode (light/dark/system) and persists the choice via SharedPreferences.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app starts, when ThemeModel initializes, then it loads the saved theme preference from SharedPreferences.
Given the user selects a dark mode option, when the selection changes, then ThemeModel notifies listeners and persists the new value.
Given the user selects 'system', when the device is in dark mode, then the app renders dark theme.

features.name: Settings Theme Toggle
features.description: Add a theme selector section to the Settings screen with Light / Dark / System options, visually indicating the current selection.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user opens Settings, when the screen renders, then a theme selector section is visible with Light/Dark/System options.
Given the user taps a theme option, when the selection changes, then the app theme updates immediately without restart.
Given a theme option is selected, when the Settings screen renders, then the selected option is visually highlighted.

features.name: App-Wide Theme Application
features.description: Update main.dart to wrap the app with the ThemeModel provider and set MaterialApp.router's theme and darkTheme properties so all screens respond to theme changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user toggles dark mode in Settings, when the theme changes, then all visible screens update to the new theme instantly.
Given the app restarts, when the user had dark mode enabled, then the app launches in dark mode.

out_of_scope: Scheduled/auto theme switching based on time of day

state_management.justification: Existing app uses Provider. Dark mode toggle is a simple boolean/enum state — no need for Riverpod migration.

navigation.pattern: Existing go_router setup — no changes needed
navigation.justification: No new screens. Only modifying Settings screen.

local_storage.justification: Existing app uses SharedPreferences. Theme preference (light/dark/system) is a simple string key-value.

di.justification: Provider IS the DI system for this app.

theme_approach.description: Add FortunaTheme.dark with Material 3 dark color scheme. Add ThemeMode to MaterialApp.router. Create ThemeModel (ChangeNotifier) with ThemeMode persistence via SharedPreferences.
theme_approach.color_strategy: Define dark variants of all FortunaColors in color_tokens.dart, then use them in FortunaTheme.dark with ColorScheme.dark().

items.title: Add dark color tokens to color_tokens.dart
items.description: Add dark theme color variants to FortunaColors class. Each existing color needs a dark variant following Material 3 dark theme guidelines.

items.title: Add dark theme to FortunaTheme
items.description: Add static ThemeData get dark method to FortunaTheme class using the new dark color tokens and ColorScheme.dark().

items.title: Create ThemeModel with persistence
items.description: Create ThemeModel (ChangeNotifier) that manages ThemeMode state (light/dark/system), persists via SharedPreferences, and loads saved preference on init.

items.title: Wire theme into main.dart with Provider
items.description: Add ThemeModel to MultiProvider in main.dart. Set MaterialApp.router theme and darkTheme properties. Support system theme via themeMode.

items.title: Add theme selector to Settings screen
items.description: Add a theme mode selector section to SettingsScreen with Light/Dark/System options. Use context.read<ThemeModel>() to change theme on selection.

criteria_results.criterion: Given the app is running, when dark mode is active, then all screens use dark surface colors
Given the app is running, when dark mode is active, then the ColorScheme.brightness is Brightness.dark
Given the app is running, when dark mode is active, then all Material 3 color tokens have appropriate dark values
FortunaTheme.dark returns a complete ThemeData with Brightness.dark
All Material 3 color tokens mapped from FortunaColors.dark* constants
Card, AppBar, FAB, buttons, inputs, dividers all have dark variants
Given the app starts, when ThemeModel initializes, then it loads the saved theme preference from SharedPreferences
Given the user selects a dark mode option, when the selection changes, then ThemeModel notifies listeners and persists the new value
Given the user selects 'system', when the device is in dark mode, then the app renders dark theme
Given the user toggles dark mode in Settings, when the theme changes, then all visible screens update to the new theme instantly
Given the app restarts, when the user had dark mode enabled, then the app launches in dark mode
Given the user opens Settings, when the screen renders, then a theme selector section is visible with Light/Dark/System options
Given the user taps a theme option, when the selection changes, then the app theme updates immediately without restart
Given a theme option is selected, when the Settings screen renders, then the selected option is visually highlighted

covers_criteria: Given the app starts, when ThemeModel initializes, then it loads the saved theme preference
Given the user selects a dark mode option, when the selection changes, then ThemeModel notifies listeners and persists
